records.ety: [OE. damosel, damesel, damisel, damsel, fr. OF. damoisele, damisele, gentlewoman, F. demoiselle young lady; cf. OF. damoisel young nobleman, F. damoiseau; fr. LL. domicella, dominicella, fem., domicellus, dominicellus, masc., dim. fr. L. domina, dominus. See Dame, and cf. Demoiselle, Doncella.]

defs: A young person, either male or female, of noble or gentle extraction; as, Damsel Pepin; Damsel Richard, Prince of Wales.
A young unmarried woman; a girl; a maiden.
quotes: With her train of damsels she was gone, In shady walks the scorching heat to shun.
Sometimes a troop of damsels glad, . . . Goes by to towered Camelot.

defs: An attachment to a millstone spindle for shaking the hopper.
